commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The PR has concrete author-fixable defects and needs maintainer sign-off plus contributor real behavior proof before merge; automation should not take over while proof is insufficient.; Needs attention: The diff has no dependency or supply-chain change, but the enabled background title path can unnecessarily disclose transcript content for sessions that already have stronger titles.; Review finding: [P2] Pref... (sha=c5e53be45452f68acd7f5e855990868b11ecf4c8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds default-off AI session title generation, persists `autoTitle`, exposes `derivedTitle` to session lists and the Control UI, and extends runtime model-auth lookup for session auth profiles.

PR surface: Source +339, Tests +245. Total +584 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defects by source inspection: the merge result prefers `derivedTitle` before `displayName`, and the generator only skips existing `autoTitle`. The linked feature request itself is a new capability rather than a bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR has concrete author-fixable defects and needs maintainer sign-off plus contributor real behavior proof before merge; automation should not take over while proof is insufficient.

target.title: fix(sessions): auto-generate session titles via AI summarization
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The session-title transcript scanner read only one 8192-byte chunk before deciding whether enough user turns existed, so a long first JSONL user record could be parsed as a truncated line and skipped.
- Solution: Stream complete transcript JSONL records in order and stop only after the configured number of user turns has been counted.
- What changed: Reused the existing transcript line streaming helper in `session-title-generator` and added a regression test with a long first user record followed by enough later user turns.
- What did NOT change: `sessionTitle.enabled` remains explicit opt-in. Manual session names and subject labels still take precedence over `autoTitle`. No dependency or lockfile changes.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Power users with long first prompts or tool-heavy opening turns can still get useful session titles once the configured turn threshold is reached, instead of keeping an unhelpful first-message fallback title.

Fixes #77165

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Session title generation must not miss the configured turn threshold when the first user JSONL record is longer than the old fixed read buffer. The PR also preserves the existing opt-in title behavior, auth-profile routing coverage, `autoTitle` persistence, and sidebar/session-list title precedence tests.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v22.22.0, current OpenClaw PR worktree at commit `c5e53be45452f68acd7f5e855990868b11ecf4c8`. Gateway startup used the daily-fix dev gateway script with an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
OPENCLAW_REPO=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
  OPENCLAW_LOG=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/openclaw-dev-gateway-current-head.log \
  OPENCLAW_DEV_AUTO_PAIR=false \
  /home/0668001030/.claude/skills/daily-fix/scripts/start_openclaw_dev_gateway.sh

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts \
  src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts \
  src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts \
  ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts

pnpm check:test-types
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs \
  src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts
git diff --check
```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
2026-06-02T17:52:22.609+08:00 [gateway] agent model: zte/nebulacoder-cot-v8.0 (thinking=off, fast=off)
2026-06-02T17:52:22.614+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 6.3s)
2026-06-02T17:52:22.624+08:00 [gateway] ready
Test Files  7 passed (7)
Tests  353 passed (353)
$ pnpm tsgo:test
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
[plugin-sdk boundary dts] fresh; skipping
[plugin-sdk package boundary dts] fresh; skipping
[qa-channel boundary dts] fresh; skipping
[discord boundary dts] fresh; skipping
[slack boundary dts] fresh; skipping
[whatsapp boundary dts] fresh; skipping
[plugin-sdk boundary root shims] fresh; skipping
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The new regression test writes a first user JSONL record over 8192 bytes, then two later user turns. On current head, title generation still calls the label generator with all three user messages and persists `autoTitle`, proving the scanner reads complete records and reaches the configured threshold. The current focused session/UI/runtime tests, test typecheck, focused oxlint for touched files, and diff whitespace check passed.
- **What was not tested:** No live Control UI/Mantis screenshot was captured. Full monorepo `pnpm lint --threads=8` did not complete in this local environment, so the submitted proof uses the official oxlint wrapper on the touched files plus current CI/PR checks after push for broad lint coverage.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- RF-002 / RF-008: Fixed the bounded JSONL scanner by reading complete transcript lines through the existing streaming transcript helper before checking the threshold.
- RF-003: Added a regression test for a long first user record followed by enough later user turns.
- RF-004 / RF-005 / RF-006 / RF-007: The config, privacy, and auth-provider boundaries are still maintainer decisions; this update keeps the feature default-off and preserves the existing focused auth/profile and precedence tests.
- RF-009 / RF-010: Re-ran the requested focused session-title, label/auth, session-utils, runtime-auth, and UI session controller tests on current head.
- RF-001: The PR was still labeled `status: ⏳ waiting on author` before this update; this commit addresses the author-side scanner finding so the label can be re-evaluated by the normal review flow.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider`, `merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary`, plus session-state and availability/message-delivery surfaces in the diff.
- **Risk explanation:** The broader PR adds a default-off `sessionTitle` config surface, durable `autoTitle` session metadata, and session-scoped model/auth routing for the background title request. This update only changes how the title generator reads transcript records before the model call; it does not widen when background title requests run or which auth profile/model they use.
- **Why acceptable:** Generation still requires `sessionTitle.enabled: true`, manual `displayName` and existing `subject` keep precedence over `autoTitle`, and the new scanner uses the repository's existing bounded transcript streaming helper rather than reading whole transcript files. Maintainers still own final sign-off for the config/privacy/auth boundary, and the PR body keeps the Mantis/Control UI visual proof gap explicit.

## Maintainer-ready confidence

- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium-high for the scanner blocker and local runtime/test evidence; medium overall because the feature remains a large PR with config/privacy/auth-provider merge-risk labels and no live Control UI/Mantis screenshot.
- **Patch quality notes:** The new commit is narrow: two touched files, no dependency changes, and the production change replaces the fixed chunk parser with an existing complete-line transcript stream. The fire-and-forget catch behavior, no-op default returns, and session-state updates are part of the existing PR design: failures do not block replies, missing config/session/transcript state leaves titles unchanged, and persisted `autoTitle` remains behind explicit opt-in generation.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Focused runtime startup, unit/regression tests, test typecheck, touched-file lint, and diff whitespace check.
- Target test file: `src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts`.
- Additional focused test files: `src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts`, `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: A long first JSONL user record does not block counting later user turns; `sessionTitle` remains opt-in; title generation carries the resolved auth profile; `autoTitle` persists; subject/manual names remain stronger than `autoTitle`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the exact scanner defect at the title-generation boundary while preserving the existing tests for config, auth routing, persistence, and sidebar/session-list precedence.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The title generator treated a fixed-size byte chunk as if it were a complete JSONL prefix. That broke the transcript invariant that each JSONL line must be parsed only after the full record has been read, so a long first user message could be skipped and the later valid turns were not enough to trigger title generation.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The scanner now consumes the same complete-line transcript stream used elsewhere in session transcript handling. That moves the decision point from arbitrary byte boundaries to JSONL record boundaries, so threshold counting is based on real user records and the symptom cannot recur for long first records.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
